NCT04751708.name: ATTENTION
NCT04751708.group: BAO within 12 h, EVT + medical vs medical alone (90-day mRS 0-3 RR)
NCT04751708.snippet: Source: Tao C et al. NEJM 2022;387:1361-1372 (ATTENTION).

evidence.label: Enrollment
evidence.text: 340 adults in China with angiographically confirmed acute basilar artery occlusion (BAO) within 12 hours of estimated onset randomized 2:1 open-label to endovascular thrombectomy + best medical management (n=226) or best medical management alone (n=114). Trial terminated early at pre-specified interim for efficacy.

evidence.label: Primary - 90-day mRS 0-3
evidence.text: Good functional outcome (mRS 0-3 at 90 days): thrombectomy 46% (104/226) vs medical 23% (26/114); adjusted risk ratio 2.06 (95% CI 1.46-2.91; P<0.001); unadjusted risk ratio 2.43. Primary endpoint met.

evidence.label: Secondary - sICH and 90-day Mortality
evidence.text: Symptomatic intracranial haemorrhage: thrombectomy 5% vs medical 0% (P=0.02). 90-day mortality: 37% vs 55% (HR 0.56, 0.40-0.79). Despite higher sICH, net clinical benefit strongly favoured thrombectomy given large mortality reduction.

evidence.label: Risk of Bias (RoB 2.0)
evidence.text: D1 LOW - central IVR stratified by site and onset time. D2 SOME CONCERNS - open-label (surgical intervention), mitigated by blinded 90-day mRS adjudication. D3 LOW - 100% ascertainment. D4 LOW - blinded mRS. D5 LOW - SAP locked.

evidence.label: Eligibility (AACT-derived)
evidence.text: AACT-derived (auto-populated 2026-05-04). Sex: ALL; min age 18 Years; healthy volunteers: f. CRITERIA: "Inclusion Criteria~1. Symptoms and signs compatible with ischemia in the basilar artery territory；~2. Basilar artery occlusion confirmed by CTA/MRA/DSA；~3. Age of 18 years or older；~4. Time from stroke onset to randomization within 12 hours of estimated time of basilar artery occlusion (defined as the sudden onset of stroke symptoms consistent with acute occlusion of the basilar artery (e.g. not considering any previous minor prodromal symptoms) as adjudicated by two local experienced neurologi...

NCT02737189.name: BAOCHE
NCT02737189.group: BAO 6-24 h, EVT + medical vs medical alone (90-day mRS 0-3 RR)
NCT02737189.snippet: Source: Jovin TG et al. NEJM 2022;387:1373-1384 (BAOCHE).

evidence.label: Enrollment
evidence.text: 217 adults with symptomatic acute BAO between 6-24 hours from last-known-well randomized 1:1 open-label to endovascular thrombectomy + best medical (n=110) or best medical alone (n=107). Chinese-only multicentre. Trial terminated early at pre-specified interim.

evidence.label: Primary - 90-day mRS 0-3
evidence.text: Good functional outcome at 90 days: thrombectomy 46% (51/110) vs medical 24% (26/107); risk ratio 1.91 (95% CI 1.29-2.82; P<0.001). Primary endpoint met.

evidence.label: Secondary - sICH, Mortality
evidence.text: Symptomatic intracranial haemorrhage: thrombectomy 6% vs medical 1% (P=0.06). 90-day mortality: 31% vs 42% (P=0.05). Overall net benefit favours thrombectomy.

evidence.label: Risk of Bias (RoB 2.0)
evidence.text: D1 LOW - central IVR. D2 SOME CONCERNS - open-label (surgical), mitigated by blinded mRS. D3 LOW - 99% ascertainment. D4 LOW - blinded adjudication. D5 LOW - SAP locked.

evidence.label: Eligibility (AACT-derived)
evidence.text: AACT-derived (auto-populated 2026-05-04). Sex: ALL; min age 18 Years; max age 80 Years; healthy volunteers: f. CRITERIA: "Inclusion Criteria:~1. Posterior circulation AIS within 6-24 hours from symptom onset/last seen well where patient is ineligible for i.v. thrombolytic treatment, or patient has received i.v. thrombolytic therapy without recanalization.~2. Occlusion (Thrombolysis in Myocardial Infarction, TIMI 0-1) of the basilar artery or intracranial segments of both vertebral arteries as evidenced by computed tomography (CT) angiography, magnetic resonance (MR) angiography or angiogram.~3. Age ≥18 and ≤80 yea...

NCT01717755.name: BASICS
NCT01717755.group: BAO <6 h, EVT + best medical vs best medical (90-day mRS 0-3 RR, did not meet superiority)

allOutcomes.title: Good functional outcome (90-day mRS 0-3) (primary, RR, superiority not met)

NCT01717755.snippet: Source: Langezaal LCM et al. NEJM 2021;384:1910-1920 (BASICS).

evidence.label: Enrollment
evidence.text: 300 adults with acute symptomatic BAO within 6 hours from symptom onset randomized 1:1 open-label at European, North-American, Asian centres to endovascular thrombectomy + best medical (n=154) or best medical alone (n=146).

evidence.label: Primary - 90-day mRS 0-3
evidence.text: Good functional outcome: thrombectomy 44% (68/154) vs medical 38% (55/146); risk ratio 1.17 (95% CI 0.89-1.54; P=0.19). Superiority not demonstrated. Absolute risk difference +6.5% (95% CI -4.0 to +16.9).

evidence.label: Secondary - sICH, Subgroups
evidence.text: Symptomatic intracranial haemorrhage: thrombectomy 4.5% vs medical 0.7%. Benefit appeared to favour severe strokes (NIHSS >=10) and centres with higher procedural volume. 90-day mortality: 38% vs 43% (NS). Trial underpowered for subgroup effects.

evidence.label: Risk of Bias (RoB 2.0)
evidence.text: D1 LOW - central IVR. D2 SOME CONCERNS - open-label + 37% protocol violations (crossovers from medical to thrombectomy). D3 LOW - 100% ascertainment. D4 LOW - blinded mRS. D5 LOW - SAP locked.

evidence.label: Eligibility (AACT-derived)
evidence.text: AACT-derived (auto-populated 2026-05-04). Sex: ALL; min age 18 Years; healthy volunteers: f. CRITERIA: "Inclusion criteria~* Symptoms and signs compatible with ischemia in the basilar artery territory.~* Basilar artery occlusion (BAO) confirmed by CTA or MRA.~* Age 18 years or older (i.e., candidates must have had their 18th birthday).~* If IVT is considered as part of best medical management, IVT should be started within 4.5 hours of estimated time of BAO. title: RapidMeta Neurology | Thrombectomy in Basilar Artery Occlusion v1.1
